title: Add login authentication, role-based access control, and admin user management

requirements.text: Add an AppUser data model to the backend with fields: id (Nat), username (Text), passwordHash (Text), and role (variant { Admin; User }). Store users in a stable HashMap. Seed one default admin account (username: 'admin', password: 'admin123') on first deploy if no users exist.

quotes: create users' names and passwords for users and admin

acceptanceCriteria: AppUser type exists with id, username, passwordHash, and role fields
Users are stored in stable state that survives upgrades
A default admin user exists after first deploy

requirements.text: Expose backend functions for user management: login(username, password) returning a session token or error; getUsers() returning all users (admin only); createUser(username, password, role) (admin only); updateUser(id, username, password, role) (admin only); deleteUser(id) (admin only).

quotes: create users' names and passwords for users and admin

acceptanceCriteria: login() returns a success result with user info on valid credentials, error on invalid
getUsers(), createUser(), updateUser(), deleteUser() are gated to admin callers only
Passwords are stored hashed (using a simple deterministic hash, not plaintext)

requirements.text: Replace the Internet Identity authentication gate in the frontend with a username/password login screen. The login screen should show the app logo/name, a username field, a password field, and a Sign In button. On successful authentication, store the logged-in user (id, username, role) in React context/state accessible app-wide.

quotes: Authentication: This is more of an internal management list
Login screen with username + password authentication

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users see only the login screen
Incorrect credentials show an error message
Successful login navigates to the dashboard
Logged-in user info (name, role) is accessible throughout the app

requirements.text: Add a Settings page accessible only to admin users. Add a 'Settings' navigation link in the Sidebar that is only rendered when the logged-in user has the Admin role. Regular users should not see or be able to navigate to the Settings page.

quotes: Settings menu location: Where should this settings menu appear — in a sidebar
should it only be accessible to admins? yes

acceptanceCriteria: Settings link appears in the sidebar only for admin users
Regular users cannot access /settings directly (redirected away)
Admin users can navigate to the Settings page

requirements.text: Build the Settings page with a User Management section. Display a table of all users showing their username and role. Provide buttons to: (1) Create a new user (opens a dialog with username, password, and role fields), (2) Edit an existing user (opens a pre-filled dialog for username, password, and role), (3) Delete a user (with a confirmation prompt). Prevent deletion of the currently logged-in admin user.

quotes: User management inside Settings: create, view, edit, and delete users with name, password, and role (admin or regular user)

acceptanceCriteria: User table lists all users with username and role columns
Create user dialog validates that username and password are non-empty
Edit user dialog pre-fills existing username and role
Delete prompts for confirmation before removing
Logged-in admin cannot delete their own account

requirements.text: Enforce role-based access control across the app. Regular users (role: User) should not see the Settings sidebar link, cannot access admin-only pages, and all admin-only action buttons (add/edit/delete) on AssetsPage, PartsInventoryPage, RepairTicketsPage, etc. remain gated. Base access checks on the locally stored logged-in user role instead of the previous Internet Identity-based role check.

quotes: Admins should have special privileges within the app, while regular users have limited access

acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only UI elements are hidden for regular users
Role gating works based on the username/password login session role
Regular users can view data but cannot create, edit, or delete records

requirements.text: Update the Header component to display the logged-in user's username and role, and replace the Internet Identity logout button with a logout action that clears the local session and returns the user to the login screen.

quotes: Login screen with username + password authentication

acceptanceCriteria: Header shows the current user's name and role badge
Logout button clears session and shows the login screen
No Internet Identity references remain visible in the UI

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts
Keep all backend logic in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
Preserve all existing features: assets, repair tickets, parts inventory, audit log, reports, clients
nonGoals: Full cryptographic password hashing (a deterministic simple hash is acceptable)
Session token expiry or refresh mechanisms
